RT @bitcoinprice: The average price of Bitcoin across all exchanges is 999.99 USD

@edenthecat I'm not arguing with you. I'm saying the language we talk about these things is really important. There are people who actively want to erase the history there to serve their own ideologies. The roots of OSS directly inform what it *is*. Your original framing ignored that for me.
@DartDClark Appreciate the tip!

@edenthecat I hear what you are saying, and I'm sorry to nitpick, but this issue is incredibly important to me. You are taking about a conversation about what OSS should be, not what it *is*. So many silicon valley bros simply don't know the history and philosophies that gave them OSS.
@dpetrohilos This is definitely what I meant
@edenthecat In addition- the socialist / capitalist dichotomy leads you further from the truth. I would describe OSS more as a demonstration of anarchism than either of those philosophies.

@edenthecat Because not acknowledging the truth of our present is a problem. I am fine with having a conversation about what the future of OSS might be, but trying to rewrite history and say it didn't come from socialist-leaning crypto punk circles is disingenuous at best.

@edenthecat Sure but we are using the systems that they designed with a clear purpose and goal. Any individual can argue their own motivations, but the systems and processes we work in have a clear and undeniable lineage.
@edenthecat Why is that a conversation? Early proponents of Open Source were pretty clear about their political ideologies and how OSS was a part. We owe our current eco system to them.
@DartDClark Where do you go?
